visual_findings.control_map_diagnostic: The diagnostic image is the expected OpenPose skeleton map and matches the prior OpenPose input role.
visual_findings.face_realism: Pass. The face, eyes, hair, skin tone, and clothing are coherent for a portrait.
visual_findings.control_map_leakage: Pass. No skeleton lines, colored bones, dots, or pose-map stains are visible in the generated image.
visual_findings.pose_and_hands_target: Fail for the v2 robustness target. Despite the upper-body/hands prompt, the output remains a close head-and-shoulders portrait with no visible hands, no tabletop, and no wrist/contact evidence.
visual_findings.prompt_compliance: Fail for visible separated hands/tabletop contact. The sample does not exercise the stated robustness gap.

defects.description: The generated image omits hands and tabletop contact, so it cannot validate the OpenPose hand/upper-body robustness objective.

notes: This is still useful evidence because it shows the existing square/head-focused request resists widening even with hand prompt terms.
A minimum follow-up should change framing/resolution and explicitly suppress close-up/headshot crops.
promotion_boundary: Local robustness attempt only. It does not certify OpenPose hands, full-body pose, or target-runtime behavior.
next_action: Run one minimum v3 local sample with taller framing, anti-close-up negatives, and hands/lower-third terms to test whether the target can be exercised.
